mnemonics: All People Seem To Need Data Processing (OSI Model Layers: Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical)
keyPoints: The end-to-end principle argues that intelligence should be kept at the endpoints of a network, not in the middle.
Packet switching breaks data into packets for efficient, shared use of network resources.
Layering provides a structured approach to network design, where each layer offers services to the layer above and uses services from the layer below.
commonMistakes: Confusing routing (finding a path) with forwarding (sending a packet on its chosen path).
Assuming the OSI 7-layer model is the architecture used for the Internet (TCP/IP uses a 4/5-layer model).
Thinking flow control and congestion control are the same thing (flow control is receiver vs. sender, congestion control is network vs. sender).
